meta.title: chron

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: The journey's opening root: a clean, well-known Greek family with one compound (asynchronous) held out for transfer. Owner review pending.

unit.label: chron
unit.mentor_note: Time hides inside more everyday words than it seems. Once you can see chron, you start noticing it everywhere.

members.meaning: arrangement of events in order of occurrence
a stop watch that records time with great accuracy
lasting a long time, of an illness
an instrument for measuring time precisely
not occurring at the same time as something else

discovery.understand_note: Chron means time, and it never hides far from the surface. Chronology arranges events across time. Chronic means an illness has settled in for a long stretch of time. A chronometer measures time down to the instant. Different words, same root, same idea underneath.

applies.prompt: Knowing chron- means time, what do you think asynchronous describes?

options.text: Not happening at the same time as something else
Happening very quickly, without any delay
